All Classes and Interfaces|All Packages

C

co.edu.unbosque.usuario - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario
Package containing the main entry point for Usuario.
co.edu.unbosque.usuario.controller - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario.controller
Package containing REST controllers for managing users (usuarios) and related authentication operations in the user management system.
co.edu.unbosque.usuario.dto - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario.dto
Package containing Data Transfer Object (DTO) classes responsible for transferring data between layers of the application, particularly for user authentication and login responses.
co.edu.unbosque.usuario.model - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario.model
Package containing entity classes responsible for representing the data model of users in the application, mapping them to the corresponding database tables.
co.edu.unbosque.usuario.repository - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario.repository
Package containing repository interfaces responsible for data persistence operations related to user management.
co.edu.unbosque.usuario.service - package co.edu.unbosque.usuario.service
Package containing service classes responsible for handling user authentication and authorization, including generation and validation of JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for secure access to application endpoints and service classes responsible for handling business logic related to user management, including creation, update, deletion of users, login verification, and validation of unique identifiers.
crear(Usuario) - Method in class co.edu.unbosque.usuario.controller.UsuarioController
Creates a new user (Usuario).
crear(Usuario) - Method in class co.edu.unbosque.usuario.service.UsuarioService
Creates a new user if the identification number (cedula) is not already taken.
crear(T) - Method in interface co.edu.unbosque.usuario.service.CRUDOperations
Create a new record for the given entity.
CRUDOperations<T> - Interface in co.edu.unbosque.usuario.service
Generic interface defining basic CRUD operations that service classes implement for their corresponding domain entities.
